capstone-tests-and-polish.label: Capstone Tests and Polish
capstone-tests-and-polish.summary: Add assertions, final output polish, and README handoff notes so the capstone feels complete.

try_capstone_tests_and_polish_sketch0.title: Add model assertions for task summaries
try_capstone_tests_and_polish_sketch0.prompt: You are polishing the Smart Task Tracker handoff. Add assertion-based checks that prove both subclasses produce the right summary text. Keep the existing models and complete the test workspace so running the project prints `model tests passed` after the assertions succeed.
try_capstone_tests_and_polish_sketch0.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the class names, method names, and file targets from the prompt as your checklist.

services_storage_py.content:
from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask


def load_tasks(path):
    tasks = []
    with open(path, "r") as file:
        rows = file.read().strip().splitlines()

    for row in rows[1:]:
        kind, title, points_text, extra, done_text = row.split(",")
        points = int(points_text)

        if kind == "bug":
            task = BugTask(title, points, extra)
        else:
            task = FeatureTask(title, points, extra)

        if done_text == "yes":
            task.mark_done()

        tasks.append(task)

    return tasks

tests_check_tasks_py.content: # TODO: add assert-based checks for validation, priority labels, and done counts

capstone_tests_and_polish_step2_service_.title: Add service assertions for counts and filtering
capstone_tests_and_polish_step2_service_.prompt: Continue from the previous working Smart Task Tracker. Build service-layer assertions that verify the tracker stores mixed task objects, counts them correctly, and filters high-priority tasks. Running the finished workspace should print `service tests passed`.

models_base_task_py.content:
from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask

bug = BugTask("Fix login", "high", 3)
feature = FeatureTask("Add export", "medium", "CSV export")

assert bug.summary() == "BUG | Fix login | priority: high | severity: 3"
assert feature.summary() == "FEATURE | Add export | priority: medium | feature: CSV export"

print("model tests passed")

0.message: The final handoff run should print a dashboard, report, and readiness message.

capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.title: Capstone polish: prove the tracker is ready to hand off
capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
Polish is not just prettier output. It is proof that the app still works after all the pieces come together.

Imagine handing off a volunteer schedule app. You would want a teammate to know:

- what files matter
- how to run it
- what output to expect
- which tests protect the important behavior

The Smart Task Tracker needs the same handoff care. Add focused assertions, generate the final report, and leave a clear README so another person can understand the project without reading every line first.
